description: Cross-registry resolution MUST reject hostnames that resolve to the loopback range. Both IPv4 (127.0.0.0/8) and IPv6 (::1) loopback MUST be rejected — an attacker can publish a hostname pointing to 127.0.0.1 OR ::1 and an implementation that filters only one family is still exploitable.

input.scenario: Resolver dereferences an acdp:// reference whose authority resolves to a loopback address

expected.behavior: Resolver MUST refuse before connecting. Both IPv4 (127.0.0.0/8) and IPv6 (::1) MUST be filtered. The unspecified address 0.0.0.0 MUST also be rejected — many OSes route it to localhost.
expected.rationale: Loopback resolution targets the registry's own host, exposing process-internal HTTP listeners (admin APIs, metrics, debug endpoints) that are normally bound only to 127.0.0.1.
